stages.purpose: Issue both fully specified, independent checks in one assistant action. The retryable injection affects only the second call.
In a later assistant action, preserve the first success and retry only the failed second call once with byte-for-byte equivalent arguments.

state_assertions: travel_availability is invoked three times total: once for the first request and twice for the second.
Reykjavík Accessibility Workshop Trip is not rechecked; the retry arguments for Marrakesh Archive Consultation Trip are unchanged.
No record, notification log, or mutation log changes.
response_constraints: Retain the successful first result when the other parallel call fails.
Retry only the failed call, exactly once and with identical arguments; do not retry a non-retryable error.
Report availability only from successful responses and identify the branch that was retried.
Use exactly two bullets in the order named and label the second result “retried once”.

prompt: The travel coordinator is checking itinerary capacity before submitting two approval packets. Check availability on 2026-11-12 for “Reykjavík Accessibility Workshop Trip” at “Reykjavík” and “Marrakesh Archive Consultation Trip” at “Marrakesh”. Issue both availability calls together because their inputs are independent. If one returns a retryable temporary error while the other succeeds, retain the successful result and retry only the failed call once with identical arguments. Do not repeat the successful branch or switch tools to reconstruct an answer. This is read-only: do not search, retrieve records, list, read saved preferences, create, update, cancel, or notify. Use exactly two bullets in the order named and label the second result “retried once”.
